game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles III: The Manhattan Project

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a turtle character, progress through 8 linear stages fighting waves of Foot Soldiers and themed enemies, defeat stage bosses, recover health via pickups, manage limited lives across checkpoint stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health/life attrition against enemy waves; risk of character death depleting shared life pool; special attack resource management (costs health except on final bar)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic TMNT characters with distinct weapons and special attacks; progress from Florida beaches through Manhattan to defeat Shredder and stop his plot
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: All-new NES-exclusive title (not an arcade port like TMNT II); character selection with weapon differentiation; dual 2-player modes with toggleable friendly fire; intentional regional differences (Famicom has accessible options menu, NES requires Konami Code); anti-piracy mechanics that nerf pirated copies

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives shared between players; depleted when energy gauge empties; can be set to infinite via debug menu; player can switch character upon death
difficulty.continues_system: Up to three continues provided (Famicom version allows cheat code for 3 or 5 continues)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression across 8 levels; no mid-stage checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via Konami Code option menu (NES version) or default options (Famicom version) allowing difficulty adjustment, stage select, and lives configuration; character switching on death provides tactical flexibility

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression linear through 8 levels: Florida beaches → Manhattan borough → Technodrome → Shredder's lair → Krang's Spaceship (final boss)
replayability_hooks: Four playable turtles with distinct weapons and special attacks
Hidden Konami Code stage select in NES version
Character switching upon death encourages different playstyles

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene establishes Shredder hijacking April's broadcast and kidnapping her
Stage progression physically advances geography: Florida → Manhattan → Technodrome → Shredder's lair → Krang's Spaceship
Boss encounters tied to cartoon/toyline villains (Dirtbag, Groundchuck, Slash, Leatherhead, Bebop, Rocksteady, Tokka, Rahzar, Super Shredder)
Title card reveals Shredder plot: turning Manhattan into floating island

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as narrative punctuation featuring iconic TMNT antagonists from animated series and toyline; each stage culminates in a thematic boss fight before advancing
notable_bosses: Tokka and Rahzar (from TMNT II film)
Shredder (standard form, becomes immortal if ROM is pirated)
Super Shredder (mutated film version, final boss)

faction_indicators: Player choice of turtle character signals personality: Leonardo (leader), Raphael (hot-head), Michelangelo (comedian), Donatello (tech expert)
Weapon differentiation (katana vs. sai vs. nunchaku vs. bo staff) creates character identity
2-player friendly fire toggle signals competitive vs. cooperative dynamic
danger_signals: Shared life pool creates tension between cooperative progress and individual performance
Special attack costs health (except final bar) forces risk/reward decisions in multiplayer
Piracy detection anti-pattern: ROM checksums disable cheats and make Shredder immortal, signaling developer intent around legitimate play
environmental_storytelling: Stage geography tracks Shredder's scheme: beginning on vacation, progressing through captured Manhattan, ending at his technological lair (Technodrome → Spaceship)
Enemy variety reflects locale: Foot Soldiers in all stages, Giant Mousers, Stone Warriors as thematic obstacles

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - TMNT III technical documentation)
wikipedia (Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles III: The Manhattan Project article)
